five_out_of_five: Zero fabricated owners, dates, task IDs, or status claims — every fact in the summary traces directly to the input data.
one_out_of_five: Multiple invented facts present; owners, dates, or statuses appear that have no basis in the input data.

five_out_of_five: Every real blocker, overdue item, and due-this-week task is surfaced; no signal in the input data is silently omitted.
one_out_of_five: More than half of the real blockers or overdue items in the input are missing from the summary.

five_out_of_five: Each flagged item names the specific task, its owner (or explicitly flags 'unassigned'), its due date, and a concrete next step or question a team lead can act on immediately.
one_out_of_five: Vague statements such as 'check in on X project' with no task-level specificity, no owner named, no due date cited.

five_out_of_five: Projects are ordered so the highest-risk items appear first; a team lead reading top-to-bottom hits the most urgent item within the first two project blocks.
one_out_of_five: On-track projects appear ahead of blocked or overdue ones; ordering is random or alphabetical with no urgency logic.

five_out_of_five: Summary is scannable in under 60 seconds, grouped coherently by project, with status labels that are visually distinct and not verbatim-repeated for every task.
one_out_of_five: Wall of text with no project grouping, repeated boilerplate per item, requires multiple re-reads to extract any actionable signal.
